# SRC016 - Ad Skepticism: The Consequences of Disbelief

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Buyer Psychology |
| Authors / source | Obermiller, Spangenberg & MacLachlan |
| Year | 2005 |
| Source type | Peer-reviewed empirical studies |
| Quality | High |
| URL | [https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/00913367.2005.10639199](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/00913367.2005.10639199) |

## Bias / limitation

Advertising skepticism research; not SaaS-specific.

## Core takeaway

Skeptical consumers discount ad claims and rely more on non-ad sources.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use to build proof paths: independent reviews, demos, docs, transparent limits.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown
### Source extraction

- Source ID: SRC016
- What it changes about our strategy:
- What it proves:
- What it does not prove:
- Ad tactic suggested:
- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
